frozen_components: Claude Sonnet 3.7 (base LLM for step generation and evaluation)
architecture.summary: RPM-MCTS is a Monte Carlo Tree Search variant for code generation that uses a knowledge base (vector-indexed historical algorithmic steps) as a process reward model to evaluate intermediate algorithmic steps, and sandbox execution feedback to detect and correct erroneous steps. The root node represents a coding problem; child nodes represent algorithmic steps. Selection uses a weighted combination of UCB and knowledge base retrieval similarity. Expansion generates diverse candidate steps, filters redundant nodes by cosine similarity, and scores them. Evaluation (simulation) generates complete code from a reasoning path, executes it in a sandbox, and reflects on failures by decomposing and debugging individual steps. Backpropagation updates node values. The final code is generated by either early termination (if passing all public tests and LLM confidence check) or by tracing the highest-value path.

biblio.title: RPM-MCTS: Knowledge-Retrieval as Process Reward Model with Monte Carlo Tree Search for Code Generation

claims.statement: RPM-MCTS achieves an average improvement of 11.90% on Qwen3-8B, 7.71% on Qwen3-235B-A22B, and 10.86% on Claude Sonnet 3.7 across all benchmarks
On the two most challenging datasets (APPS-competition and CodeContests), Qwen3-8B achieves an average improvement of 13.3%, Qwen3-235B-A22B achieves 14.67%, and Claude Sonnet 3.7 achieves 18.34%
RPM-MCTS achieves approximately 15% reduction in token consumption compared to SRA-MCTS
Ablation study (w/o KB) shows overall performance decreased by average of 1.05% without knowledge base retrieval, with largest decline of 4.67% on the two most challenging datasets
Ablation study (w/o ER, removing execution rewards) resulted in the largest overall performance drop

Fine-tuning Doubao-1.5-pro-32K with 2.4k samples of RPM-MCTS-generated data combined with 170k baseline samples improves MBPP+ by 1.3 points, LiveCodeBench by 4.3 points, Aider by 4.9 points, and McEval by 3.7 points

RPM-MCTS exhibits better performance than SRA-MCTS even with a rollout of 1, because it enjoys proactive guidance via knowledge base during selection and wrong step truncation with rethink-based regeneration during simulation, allowing verification and reflection and generation of complete code in a single rollout
A limitation of RPM-MCTS is that code solvable in a single line may be divided into multiple lines due to the step-by-step approach, without impacting correctness
